message.content:
Remove the `FastifyThrottlerGuard` import and `.overrideGuard(FastifyThrottlerGuard).useValue({ canActivate: () => true })` chain from these 4 e2e test files. The guard now handles test detection internally so the override is no longer needed.

Files to edit:
1. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/test/auth.module.e2e-spec.ts`
2. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/test/company.module.e2e-spec.ts`
3. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/test/user.module.e2e-spec.ts`
4. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend/test/invitation.module.e2e-spec.ts`

For each file:
- Remove the import line: `import { FastifyThrottlerGuard } from '@modules/auth/guards/fastify-throttler.guard';`
- Change the module setup from:
  ```
  moduleFixture = await Test.createTestingModule({
    imports: [AppModule],
  })
    .overrideGuard(FastifyThrottlerGuard)
    .useValue({ canActivate: () => true })
    .compile();
  ```
  To:
  ```
  moduleFixture = await Test.createTestingModule({
    imports: [AppModule],
  }).compile();
  ```

Use the Read tool first to see exact content, then Edit to make precise changes. Do not change anything else.

content.text:
All 4 files have been updated. Here's a summary of what was changed in each:

- **`auth.module.e2e-spec.ts`** — Removed the `FastifyThrottlerGuard` import line; collapsed the 4-line `createTestingModule(...).overrideGuard(...).useValue(...).compile()` chain to a single `.compile()` call.
- **`company.module.e2e-spec.ts`** — Same two changes.
- **`user.module.e2e-spec.ts`** — Same two changes.
- **`invitation.module.e2e-spec.ts`** — Same two changes (this file used `const moduleFixture: TestingModule` inline rather than a pre-declared variable, which was preserved exactly).

No other content was touched in any of the files.
